items.q: What does utilitarianism say is right action?
items.correct: The action that produces the greatest happiness for the greatest number — judged by consequences, not intentions or rules.
items.misconception: Whatever makes you personally happy is the right thing to do.
items.random_wrong: Utilitarianism says you should always tell the truth.
items.why: Students reduce utilitarianism to egoism. It is impartial — your own happiness counts no more than anyone else's. Personal happiness alone misses the aggregative core.

items.q: What does the term jihad mean in Islam?
items.correct: Struggle or striving — the greater jihad is the internal spiritual struggle against sin; the lesser jihad refers to defensive military struggle under strict conditions.
items.random_wrong: Jihad is the Islamic word for prayer.
items.why: The 'holy war' translation is reductive and reflects media usage, not Islamic teaching. The primary meaning is spiritual struggle — military jihad is tightly constrained in classical Islamic law.

items.q: What is substance dualism about mind and body?
items.correct: The view (associated with Descartes) that mind and body are two distinct kinds of substance — a non-physical mind interacting with a physical body.
items.misconception: Substance dualism is the belief that humans have two personalities.
items.random_wrong: Substance dualism means men and women are different.
items.why: Students misread 'dualism' as referring to two of anything. It specifically refers to two kinds of substance (mental and physical) — a metaphysical position with deep philosophical implications.

items.q: Which encyclical banned Catholic contraception in 1968?
items.why: Correct answer: Humanae Vitae. “Rerum Novarum” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Maximum wives allowed under Islamic law?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 wives. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
